$comment: P2 — Vercel Firewall rules spec. Consolidated to 3 rules to fit the Hobby-plan custom-rule cap. Rules 1 and 2 overlap intentionally (e.g. /wp-admin/install.php matches both); the firewall short-circuits on first match, so duplication is harmless and gives us redundant coverage. Apply via Vercel dashboard (Project → Settings → Firewall → Custom Rules) or the REST API. Re-export after each dashboard change so this file stays in sync.

rules.name: block-extensions-and-dotfiles
rules.description: Broadest single rule — denies any path containing tell-tale credential/source/script extensions: .php, .asp, .aspx, .jsp, .cgi, .env, .git, .svn, .htaccess, .htpasswd. The app is Next.js and has zero legitimate paths in those families. Catches /wp-admin/install.php (observed 2026-05-17), /xmlrpc.php, /.env, /.git/config, etc.

rules.name: block-bot-prefixes
rules.description: Denies bot/scanner path prefixes that don't necessarily carry a tell-tale extension. Required because rule 1 alone wouldn't catch /cmd_sco (no extension) or /wp-admin/ (directory probes). Catches /cmd_sco (observed 2026-05-17), /wp-admin/, /wp-content/uploads/..., /phpmyadmin/, /adminer.php, /vendor/phpunit/...

rules.name: rate-limit-public-pages
rules.description: Caps aggressive scrapers on the three public pages without blocking humans. Logs on 2026-05-17 showed 5×/sec /privacy bursts from a scraper grabbing sub-resources per page load. 30 req / 60 s per IP is well above any human pattern.
